title: hieracium epimedium subsp. glaucellum
intro: Hieracium epimedium subsp. glaucellum, also known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Its leaves and stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which help to alleviate symptoms of fever, kidney stones, and urinary tract infections. The plant's leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, gout, and digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, Hieracium epimedium subsp. glaucellum is typically dried and used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although its use should be limited due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
